findings.evidence: `rg` shows the proof at line 74 is just `rfl`; Mathlib defines `equivSubgroupOrbitsQuotientGroup` with `invFun := fun q ↦ q.liftOn' (fun g ↦ ⟦g⁻¹ • x⟧) ...` in `mathlib/Mathlib/GroupTheory/GroupAction/Quotient.lean:357`, with no existing simp lemma for this representative computation.
findings.fix: Add/use an explicit lemma for `MulAction.equivSubgroupOrbitsQuotientGroup.symm (QuotientGroup.mk g)` and prove this deck lemma by rewriting through that lemma and `subgroupFiberOrbitClass_eq_mk`, localizing the Mathlib convention instead of exposing raw defeq.
findings.issue: `regularSubgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup_symm_mk` is proved by `rfl` after installing instances, so the public deck-facing simp theorem depends on the exact internal definition of `MulAction.equivSubgroupOrbitsQuotientGroup` and the `subgroupFiberOrbitClass` wrapper.

summary: One public simp lemma rests directly on implementation-level definitional equality of the Mathlib orbit-quotient equivalence. Factor that convention into an explicit representative lemma instead of making downstream deck API depend on `rfl` across wrappers.
